project.name: TextToCAE_GearMeshDynamics
project.prompt: Simulate two meshing spur gears rotating under transmitted torque and inspect tooth contact dynamics.

connection.message: Generated transient spur gear meshing preview with rotating teeth and contact stress contours.

loads.description: Driver gear prescribed at 900 rpm; driven gear rotates at -540.0 rpm under 1800 N mm torque
loads.contact: hard normal contact, 0.08 mm backlash, 20 deg pressure angle

value.en: 18 tooth driver meshes with 30 tooth driven gear, module 2.5 mm, 12 mm face width
value.zh: 18 齿主动轮与 30 齿从动轮啮合，模数 2.5 mm，齿宽 12 mm

value.en: Opposite rotation with speed ratio equal to tooth-count ratio
value.zh: 两齿轮反向转动，速比由齿数比确定

value.en: Stress is concentrated near the theoretical line-of-action mesh zone and varies through each tooth engagement
value.zh: 应力集中在理论啮合线附近，并随单齿啮合周期变化

workflow.detail: The request is mapped to a pair of spur gears with prescribed rotational speed, torque transfer, backlash, contact zone, and transient frames.
The Abaqus script defines parametric spur gear outlines, rigid-body reference points, explicit dynamics, prescribed driver rotation, driven gear coupling, and contact-ready surfaces.
The included visual refresh script generated a deterministic transient preview. Abaqus can be run from the command panel to replace it with solver ODB data.
The result mesh contains rotating gear frames, tooth contact stress contours, angular velocity history, and mesh-force estimates.
